name: Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst
tagline: The military's only tri-service joint base, a major air-mobility hub in central New Jersey.
_note: Tri-service joint base (Burlington/Ocean County, central NJ; near Trenton/Philadelphia). CONUS full template (BAH MHA NJ204 JB McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). Combines McGuire (Air Force, 87th Air Base Wing / air mobility), Fort Dix (Army, training & reserve), and Lakehurst (Navy, NAWCAD). Only AF/Army/Navy joint base. isJoint() picks it up via name. Medical: 87th Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (jbmdl.jb.mil / af.mil, 87 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). New Jersey is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 08015 (Browns Mills).

data.Branch: U.S. Air Force (lead), U.S. Army, and U.S. Navy
data.Location: Central New Jersey (Burlington/Ocean County, near Trenton)
data.Distinction: The military's only tri-service joint base
data.Made up of: McGuire (Air Force), Fort Dix (Army), and Lakehurst (Navy)
data.Mission: Air mobility (airlift and air refueling) plus Army training and Navy engineering
data.Setting: Between Philadelphia and the Jersey Shore

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst is the military's only tri-service joint base, combining an Air Force installation (McGuire), an Army post (Fort Dix), and a Navy facility (Lakehurst). McGuire's 87th Air Base Wing anchors a major air-mobility mission of airlift and air refueling.

In central New Jersey between Philadelphia and the Jersey Shore, it's a large, multi-service community with a wide range of units and a long aviation history (Lakehurst is the historic site of the Hindenburg).

sections.title: Central Jersey Living, With Cities and Shore Nearby

data.subtitle: The base sits in central New Jersey within reach of Philadelphia, Princeton, and the shore. The main choice is which surrounding town fits your commute, schools, and budget.
intro: New Jersey costs more than many inland states, but central Jersey is more moderate than the close-in NYC/Philly suburbs, and on-base housing is available across the joint base.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across the Burlington and Ocean County towns, with Philadelphia, Princeton, and the Jersey Shore all reachable.

recommendations.advice: Wrightstown, Browns Mills, and the nearby Burlington County towns keep the commute short.
Towns toward Mount Laurel, Medford, and the better-rated school districts are popular with families.
On-base housing is available across the joint base; weigh it against the local market.
Philadelphia, Princeton, the Jersey Shore, and even NYC are all within reach for trips.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center. The joint base has multiple gates across its three sites.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Joint Base MDL site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Joint Base MDL has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods spanning the McGuire, Dix, and Lakehurst areas.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA NJ204 (JB McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. North Hanover, Pemberton, and other Burlington and Ocean County districts serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via the regional network (Philadelphia/central NJ)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and in central New Jersey the call depends on tour length and the higher-tax market.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn central Jersey before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
You've found a town and commute you're confident about.
You're comfortable with New Jersey's property taxes (which run high).
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Central Jersey is more moderate than the close-in metros, but New Jersey property taxes are high, so weigh that with your tour length. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability across the joint base and get the office's read on the local towns before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and New Jersey vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (North Hanover, Pemberton, or another local district) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the 87th Medical Group clinic or network providers.

data.a: It's the military's only tri-service joint base, combining McGuire (Air Force), Fort Dix (Army), and Lakehurst (Navy). McGuire anchors a major air-mobility mission.
On-base housing is available across the joint base; many live off base in Burlington and Ocean County towns. Wrightstown and Browns Mills are closest.
Philadelphia, Princeton, the Jersey Shore, and even New York City are all within reach for trips.
JB MDL is MHA NJ204. Use the calculator above for your grade.
